T0125.title: Feeding the Pig
T0125.definition: A term used by fraud rings to describe the process of inducing victims to keep pouring in money in order to extract maximum value from them.
T0125.description: In pig-butchering or fake investment scams, once a victim starts making small trial investments, operators exploit their greed or emotional attachment to fabricate reasons for increasing the stakes. This process is likened to fattening a pig before slaughter, with the goal of maximizing the final payout. Victims often drain their life savings or even borrow heavily during this “fattening” phase.
T0125.usageExample: Don’t close the deal yet; keep fattening him up—I think he can still borrow a lot more from his relatives.
